messages.text: How can I help with your doc?
create a prd for project ollie

I have created a comprehensive Product Requirement Document (PRD) for **Project Ollie** (formerly known as Project Friday / Work by Workspace) based on your context files and daily sync notes. 

This PRD covers:
* **Product Vision & Core Problem Statement**: Aligning with the need for an AI-native multiplayer workspace.
* **Key Principles**: Keeping human-in-the-loop task orchestration central.
* **Detailed Feature Requirements**: Including the stream-level organization, agent-driven task planning, the canvas execution model, and the intelligent homepage/feed.
* **Technical Constraints & Milestones**: Drawing from the team's engineering syncs and the July 16, 2026 "Teamfood" target.

Please let me know if you would like me to expand on any specific section, such as the detailed technical integration requirements for Google Workspace or the target user personas from the recent UXR sprint!

sandboxFiles.name: [UX Research plan] Project Ollie: Baseline Workflow & Agent Delegation
sandboxFiles.content:
Core Objective
To map the data trails, tool ecosystems, and context-sharing habits of knowledge workers in the real world, as well as their desires and expectations regarding AI delegation.
Participants
* Location & Availability: Must currently live in the U.S. and be able to attend a 90-minute, in-person study session at the NYC Google Office between July 6th and July 24th with their own laptop.1
* Company Profile: Employed at a private educational institute or a commercial business/organization (excluding public education, nonprofits, and government).1
* Software & Tool Usage:
   * Their company must use Google Workspace (Gmail, Drive, Docs, or Calendar) with official work email addresses.1
   * The "actual work" they do must involve co-editing shared documents and spreadsheets (such as Google Docs or Sheets). Users who primarily manage tasks in dedicated project management tools (like Jira, Asana, or Trello) are disqualified.1
* Role & Authority: Must have a role in their business's decision to purchase digital tools and software (either as a sole decision-maker, part of a collective group, or as an influencer).1
* Collaboration Level: They must spend at least half or more of their work week collaborating with others on projects, tasks, or shared goals.1
Details here: Ollie current needs and painpoints 


Research Questions 
* RQ1 (The Fragmented Truth): What does a knowledge worker's true "to-do list" actually look like across formal tools (Jira, Asana) and informal channels (Slack stars, open browser tabs, mental notes)?
* RQ2 (Context Breadcrumbs): When a user moves a task forward, what specific artifacts (links, documents, specific chat sentences) represent the "proof of work" that Ollie will eventually need to ingest?
* RQ3 (The Translation Gap): How do users naturally summarize a complex day of work into a project update? What details do they filter out, and what do they consider critical?
* RQ4 (Agent Delegation Triggers): What tasks do users actually want to hand off versus what they want to keep control over? What parameters (triggers, boundaries, outputs) define a successful Sojo Agent assignment?
Session script
Artifact & Task Audit (15 mins)
* Goal: Uncover the hidden, informal task ecosystems.
* Activity: Have them open their laptop and show you their current "active stack."
* Prompts to use:
   * "Show me the 3 things you know you have to do today but aren't written down in a formal project board."
   * "Look at your open browser tabs or flagged emails right now—what tasks do those represent?"
   * "Where is the conversation happening about your top priority right now? Can you show me that Slack thread/email chain?"
The "Human Ollie" Walkthrough (25 mins)
* Goal: Treat the user's memory and screen as the database to see what Ollie should have done.
* Activity: Walk backward through their last 24–48 hours of work. Have them literally point at things on their screen.
* Prompts to use:
   * "You opened this Google Doc at 10 AM and left a comment. If Ollie was a smart assistant watching this, what is the 'core project update' it should extract from that action?"
   * "If an AI wrote an update based purely on this Slack thread you just showed me, what would it miss? What's the unwritten context here?"
   * "Look at this completed task. What were all the scattered pieces of data (spreadsheets, links, client feedback) that were required to get it done?"
AI Agents tasks (15 mins)
* Goal: Use their real data to define the scope and boundaries of the agents.
* Activity: Review the real tasks identified in Phase 1 and 2. Print out blank "Sojo Agent Job Description" cards (or use a digital whiteboard) and fill them out together.
* Prompts to use:
   * "Look at this specific task you spent an hour on yesterday. If an AI Agent could have done it, what is the exact trigger to start it? (e.g., 'When a new CSV lands in this folder' or 'When a client pings me via email')."
   * "What does the agent need access to on your laptop to successfully do this?"
   * "What is the exact output you expect? A draft email? A spreadsheet update? And where should it put it?"

sandboxFiles.name: Project Ollie PRD.doc
sandboxFiles.content:
# Product Requirement Document (PRD): Project Ollie

## 1. Executive Summary

Project Ollie is an AI-native, multiplayer work surface designed natively for human-to-agent and agent-to-agent collaboration. Traditional productivity suites are built for single players or exclusively human-to-human collaboration. As workflows transition toward agentic orchestration, Project Ollie provides a structured, real-time cooperative hub—a new System of Record where teams and AI agents manage, delegate, execute, and verify work side-by-side.

---

## 2. Problem Statement & Market Opportunity

### The Problem
* **Siloed Agent Workflows:** Agents currently operate in isolated, single-player chat interfaces. Consequently, other team members lack visibility into agent outputs, progress, or context.
* **Fragmented Process Management:** Organizing, tracking, and updating work streams across multiple disparate tools requires high overhead, manual synchronization, and constant context switching.
* **Friction in Handoffs:** No natural, intuitive UX paradigm exists to transfer tasks seamlessly back and forth between humans and agents, or between multiple specialized agents.
* **Outdated Legacy Tools:** Current collaboration suites were designed prior to the agentic era, limiting their ability to support autonomous task execution.

### The Opportunity
* To establish the definitive hub for human-agent collaboration at scale. By embedding AI natively within Workspace streams, Ollie shifts AI from an isolated chat assistant to an active, collaborative teammate.

---

## 3. Product Philosophy & Guiding Principles

* **Human-Centric Paradigm:** Humans remain the ultimate orchestrators. Task assignment, approval gates, and final execution verify that agents serve to amplify human productivity rather than replace human oversight.
* **Frictionless Simplicity:** Minimize the manual tax of updating statuses and organizing work. Use automation and inference to draft project scaffolds and keep context fresh.
* **Context Preservation:** All activities, files, chats, and task histories must live within dedicated work streams to prevent loss of context and ensure agents remain aligned with project goals.
* **Actionable Transparency:** Clear attribution of work is essential. Every contribution made by an agent must be auditable, traceable back to its originating prompt, and easily editable by team members.

---

## 4. Core User Journey & Functional Requirements

The Ollie journey is divided into four primary stages: Planning, Orchestration, Execution, and Feed.

### 4.1 Planning
The foundation of any project in Ollie is the "Stream," which maintains context and aligns team members.

* **Stream Establishment:** Users can quickly spin up a dedicated stream (a project or space). The stream acts as the shared context repository.
* **Context Assembly:** Team members can drop files, notes, data sources, and links directly into the stream. Collaborators can build and refine this shared context together in real-time.
* **Stream-Scoped Conversations:** Ollie provides private, stream-scoped chat threads for individual team members. This allows users to brainstorm and instruct the agent privately without cluttering the shared stream activity.

### 4.2 Orchestration
Orchestration transforms unstructured inputs into structured, actionable project plans.

* **Agent-Driven Task Scaffolding:** Using the accumulated context, the Ollie agent proactively proposes a structured plan containing specific tasks.
* **Intelligent Assignment Suggestions:** The agent suggests whether tasks should be assigned to specific team members, to the agent itself, or to specialized sub-agents.
* **Private Review & Approval Gate:** Requesters can edit, reject, or approve the agent's proposed plan in private. Once approved, the plan is published to the shared team list.
* **Stream Status Summary:** A high-level, real-time progress bar and status summary is pinned to the top of the stream workspace.

### 4.3 Execution
Work is performed transparently within the stream workspace, utilizing collaborative interfaces.

* **Collaborative Canvas & Artifact Generation:** When an agent creates an output (e.g., a slide deck, a document, code), it displays in an interactive canvas view. Team members can leave comments or directly edit the artifact.
* **Iterative Refinement:** The agent reads comment activity on the canvas and dynamically updates the artifact in real-time based on human feedback.
* **Attribution Margins:** Hovering over any section of a generated artifact surfaces teammate or agent avatars. Clicking an avatar reveals the prompt and author behind that contribution, providing end-to-end accountability.
* **Handoff & Re-delegation:** When stuck, agents can automatically reassign tasks back to humans with clear descriptions of what blocked them.

### 4.4 Feed & Intelligent Homepage
The homepage functions as a personalized operational hub that keeps users organized across all active streams.

* **"Your Day" Editorial Summary:** A generated morning summary highlighting major daily themes, upcoming meetings, outstanding pings, and suggested actions.
* **"Take the Next Step" Recommendations:** Contextual recommendations (e.g., "Robert asked for UX examples. [Ask Ollie to research] [Ask Simon to help]").
* **Actionable Task Feed:** A unified list allowing users to reassign tasks, approve agent requests, mark items complete, or supply additional context directly from the homepage.

---

## 5. Technical Requirements & Considerations

* **Workspace Deep Integration:** Ollie must deeply integrate with Google Drive, Gmail, Calendar, and Chat, allowing background context retrieval and outbound action execution.
* **Data Sandboxing & Privacy Guardrails:** To prevent data leakage, Ollie must sandbox enterprise data. Users must explicitly define data sources and connectors. Robust security audits are required prior to expanding access outside immediate development teams.
* **Ecosystem Compatibility:** Ollie must support external agent execution, allowing custom enterprise agents (e.g., coding agents, security auditing agents) to plug into the orchestration layer.

---

## 6. Release & Validation Plan

### Phase 1: Prototype Testing & UXR (June 2026)
* Run rapid prototyping cycles focusing on the "Stream" and "Task" primitive concepts.
* Conduct user research sprints with external Workspace customers and internal teams to analyze baseline workflows, context-sharing habits, and agent delegation expectations.

### Phase 2: Teamfood Milestones (Target: July 16, 2026)
* **Coding Agent:** Enable end-to-end software iteration tasks.
* **Agent Quality & Scheduling:** Stabilize background scheduled jobs and resolve chat streaming latency bugs.
* **Data Privacy Audit:** Execute a security audit to prepare Ollie for internal Directors+ testing.
* **UX/UI Refinements:** Ship the updated homepage feed, simplified streams, and canvas interaction controls.

### Phase 3: General Dogfooding (H2 2026)
* Expand internal use cases to gradually replace recurring sync rituals with automated, Ollie-managed status boards and asynchronous handoffs.
